commands.review_summary:
This PR chmods the bundled OpenAI Whisper and video-frame shell helpers to executable, adds release-check/test coverage for bundled skill shell script modes, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.5.18 record the docs-invoked helpers as 100644, which matches the permission-denied failure mode. I did not execute a helper in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the focused PR already contains the code change and no actionable review defect remains; maintainers should use normal CI and merge gates.

target.title: fix(skills): restore executable bit on bundled whisper script + release-time check
target.body:
## Problem

The bundled Whisper skill shell script (`skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh`) lost its executable bit at some point, causing `EACCES` failures when the skill tries to run it (fixes #9303).

## Solution

- Restore the executable bit (`chmod +x`) on `transcribe.sh`
- Add a release-time check (`collectSkillShellScriptExecutableErrors`) that scans all `skills/*/scripts/*.sh` files and fails the build if any are missing the executable bit — prevents this class of regression permanently

## Testing

- 10 tests pass including 2 new tests for the executable-bit checker (non-executable detection + executable acceptance)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Bundled OpenAI Whisper and video-frame skill helper scripts must be executable so direct helper invocation does not fail with EACCES.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head 2204a78702 on macOS with bash/zsh, Node, curl, and Homebrew ffmpeg. The Whisper request used a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP stub so no audio or API key left the machine.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Verified git modes and execute bits, ran `skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh` against the local stub, generated a tiny video with ffmpeg, then ran `skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh` to extract a frame.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the PR checkout:

```text
$ git rev-parse --short HEAD
2204a78702
$ git ls-tree HEAD skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh
100755 blob 2a4fd20afa6104f0624a6f60ed82e4a81671ec1d	skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh
100755 blob 31b3adb34c068732d04cb0d951d519338458df98	skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh
$ test -x skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh && test -x skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh && echo executable
executable
$ OPENAI_API_KEY=redacted OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:18743/v1 skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh $tmp/sample.wav --out $tmp/transcript.txt
/var/folders/wk/9gjf5c_940g4npdlmg0r3h8c0000gn/T/tmp.9p5hDDgFuQ/transcript.txt
$ sed -n '1p' $tmp/transcript.txt
transcribed by local proof endpoint
$ command -v ffmpeg
/opt/homebrew/bin/ffmpeg
$ ffmpeg -hide_banner -loglevel error -y -f lavfi -i testsrc=size=32x32:rate=1:duration=1 $tmp/sample.mp4
$ skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh $tmp/sample.mp4 --index 0 --out $tmp/frame.jpg
/var/folders/wk/9gjf5c_940g4npdlmg0r3h8c0000gn/T/tmp.9p5hDDgFuQ/frame.jpg
$ file $tmp/frame.jpg
/var/folders/wk/9gjf5c_940g4npdlmg0r3h8c0000gn/T/tmp.9p5hDDgFuQ/frame.jpg: JPEG image data, JFIF standard 1.02, aspect ratio, density 1x1, segment length 16, comment: "Lavc62.11.100", baseline, precision 8, 32x32, components 3
$ test -s $tmp/frame.jpg && echo frame-created
frame-created
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both helper scripts are recorded as 100755 and pass `test -x`; the Whisper helper wrote the expected transcript text from the local endpoint; the video-frame helper wrote a non-empty 32x32 JPEG frame.
- **What was not tested:** A live OpenAI API transcription request was not sent; the local OpenAI-compatible endpoint was used to exercise the helper's direct execution and upload path without external data transfer.
target.author: jackal092927
